title: gymnogramma consanguinea
intro: Gymnogramma consanguinea, commonly known as the False Daisy or Gymnogramma, is a plant species in the family Compositae. It is a flowering plant characterized by its daisy-like appearance and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Gymnogramma consanguinea is often used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, and is typically consumed in the form of tea or decoction.
